data.title: Manta Lianchuang: Market makers are all parasites. If there is a real demand, they can only lend no more than 0.2% of the coins
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Manta Network co-founder Victor Ji wrote on X: "Every day we receive invitations from so-called active market makers and OTCs to buy coins and acquire them. My attitude is to stay away. Whether market makers are active or passive, they are all blood-sucking worms in my eyes. They don't look at the fundamentals of the project at all, but every time there is a meeting, these people are very active in organizing events and running meetings, and the boss is also rich. Why? All this money comes from the project community. If in this industry, the more funds do not pay attention to fundamentals, the faster the industry will collapse, and market makers are the most blatant group of people who do not pay attention to fundamentals. I believe that liquidity comes from real community transactions. You are optimistic or bearish, both are natural markets. If market makers are willing to participate, you can buy coins in the market to get positions." Victor Ji added: "If some project founders are worried about insufficient liquidity and are unwilling to pay to be retainers, then my suggestion is to do loans, but the size must be the smallest. Our earliest calamari in the Polkadot era was asked by Sanjian market making for more than 3% Token, these grandsons turned around and sold the coins, and told us how legit they were and would never sell the coins. The real loan only needs no more than 0.2% of the coins. Because think about it, if the depth of 2% up and down needs 200k, it is already a lot, and 0.2% of the coins must be more than this value. If the market maker wants more coins, isn’t it just to crash the market? "

data.title: Analysis: Ethereum’s nearly 20% plunge breaks bull trendline from 2022 Terra crash
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News TradingView data shows that the price of Ethereum plunged nearly 20% in the seven days ending March 9, marking the largest weekly percentage drop since November 2022. The sell-off broke the bull trend line that began from the lows after the collapse of Terra algorithmic stablecoin UST in June 2022, which led to the loss of billions of investor wealth. This decisive drop means that Ethereum's nearly three-year bull trend may have ended, and the focus turns to deeper losses, possibly falling to support near the September-October 2023 low, around $1,500. Trendlines help visualize the direction of trader funds and where prices may change. An ascending or bull trendline represents a level where demand is expected to be sufficient to avoid further price declines. When a long-term bullish trendline is broken, as in the case of ETH, it indicates weakening demand, indicating a possible bearish shift in market trends. Breaking through a trendline often prompts other traders to sell, resulting in greater losses. (CoinDesk)

data.title: UXLINK OAOG (One Account One Gas) protocol is launched
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: UXLINK, a social infrastructure project, announced on the official X platform that its OAOG (One Account One Gas) protocol has been officially launched.
With UXLINK OAOG, users only need one social account and one GAS token (UXLINK) to seamlessly interact between various blockchains. This innovation simplifies the Web3 experience, enables smoother transactions, and enhances user control.
The head of the UXLINK community said that we have been committed to allowing more ordinary users to actively participate in Web3 interactions, but the previous high threshold severely limited user participation. Through the OAOG protocol, users have obtained a smooth interactive experience, convenient account management and better security, which will effectively promote the development of large-scale Web3 applications and help the development of the industry.

data.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary: Tariff strategy will drive $1.3 trillion in investment and boost U.S. economic growth
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick denied the risk of a recession and said Trump's tariff strategy would drive $1.3 trillion in investment and unleash the potential for U.S. economic growth. Despite Lutnick's optimism, cryptocurrency traders are less confident, with Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies falling, and Polymarket's forecast of the probability of a U.S. recession in 2025 rising to 41%. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Adam Back: The US Bitcoin strategic reserve is of great significance and provides an authoritative reference for institutional funds to enter the market
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Adam Back, co-founder of Bitcoin infrastructure development company Blockstream, wrote in X: "The impact of the US Bitcoin strategic reserve statement has been underestimated, and it has far-reaching significance for the social acceptance of Bitcoin. This statement marks that the US government is actually promoting the development of Bitcoin and provides a new authoritative reference for sovereign wealth funds, pension funds and mutual funds to allocate Bitcoin."

data.title: In the past 90 days, only BERA, BGB and other 17 top 100 tokens outperformed BTC
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CoinMarketCap market data shows that among the top 100 tokens by market value, only 17 tokens have increased more than BTC in the past 90 days, including BERA (+499%), IP (+81%), BGB (+78%), etc. BTC's performance during the same period was -15%.
It is understood that the Altcoin Season Index is a new function launched by CoinMarketCap to measure the popularity of altcoins. It counts how many tokens in the top 100 assets by market value have outperformed BTC in market performance. If the value is about 75, the market is more likely to be in the Altcoin Season, and if it is below 25, it is likely to be in the Bitcoin Season. Since February 27, the index has been below 25 for 12 consecutive days.

data.title: Planet Lunch News
EU Plans To Launch Digital Euro CBDC In October
data.description: According to BlockBeats, European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde has announced that the European Union intends to introduce the digital euro CBDC in October this year.

data.title: Japan Approves Reforms In Crypto Brokerage And Stablecoin Laws
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Japanese Cabinet has approved a proposal to reform laws related to cryptocurrency brokerage and stablecoins. As announced by the Financial Services Agency (FSA) of Japan, the government has sanctioned a Cabinet resolution to amend the Payment Services Act.

The new legislation will allow cryptocurrency companies to operate as 'intermediary businesses.' This change means that brokers will no longer need to apply for the same type of licenses required by crypto trading platforms and wallet operators. Additionally, the bill provides stablecoin issuers with greater flexibility regarding the types of assets that can back their tokens.

Historically, no crypto-related legal changes have been rejected by the Japanese Parliament after receiving Cabinet approval. Similarly, the Cabinet has never dismissed any legal change proposals concerning Japanese crypto regulations put forward by the FSA. The FSA holds a significant degree of authority in handling crypto regulatory matters in Japan.

data.title: DRB rose above 0.00019 USDT. Since its listing on LBank, the maximum increase has reached 228%.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News LBank market shows: DRB rose above 0.00019 USDT, reaching a maximum of 0.00019858 USDT, and is now quoted at 0.00018 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 141%. Since the launch of LBank, the current maximum increase has reached 228%, and the current market value is about 18 million US dollars.
